system: Cultures can merge, split, revive, assimilate or become court cultures.
Species communities can migrate, be granted charters, be expelled, be enslaved, be liberated or found states.
Religions can split, reform, syncretize or radicalize.
Mage institutions can grow, collapse, become corrupt, be outlawed or take political power.
Languages and place names can change over centuries.
Old claims can expire, become myths or be revived by ambitious rulers.
Dynasties can die out, continue through adoption, return from exile or be forged.
Cities can grow, burn, decline, become capitals, plague graves or sacred sites.
Trade routes can shift because of war, ports, road decay, piracy, climate, plague or technology.
Historians can disagree about whether magic, betrayal, disease, logistics or divine judgment caused an event.
